title: Passing Data In (Parameters) 📥
description: Feed your functions with the custom data they need.

slides.title: Feeding the Machine 📥
slides.content:
# Parameters

A machine that does the *exact* same thing every single time is boring. What if we want `sayHello` to greet a *specific* person?

We can feed data into the parentheses! This data is called a **parameter**.

```swift
func greet(name: String) {
    print("Hello, \(name)!")
}
```
Here, `name` is a parameter of type `String`.

quizData.question: What is a parameter in a function?

options.text: The final keyword that ends the function.
Data that is passed INSIDE the function so the function can use it.
The name of the function itself.

quizData.explanation: Parameters act as placeholders or input slots where you can insert custom data when you call the function.

slides.title: Calling with Parameters 📞
slides.content:
# Providing the Data

When you call a function that requires a parameter, Swift forces you to provide it! You also must type the parameter's name.

```swift
// Calling the function created earlier
greet(name: "Tim")
greet(name: "Alice")
```
It's very safe: If `greet` expects a String, and you try to pass `greet(name: 123)`, Swift throws an error before the app even runs!

slides.title: Multiple Parameters 👥

quizData.question: If a function is defined as `func add(a: Int, b: Int)`, how do you correctly call it?
quizData.explanation: In normal Swift functions, you must provide the parameter names followed by a colon when calling it: `add(a: 5, b: 5)`.

slides.title: See It in Action
slides.content: Change the parameters in the function call to see the output change!
